title: Lindernia ruelloides
intro: Lindernia ruelloides, also known as waterwort, is a small, aquatic plant species belonging to the Linderniaceae family. The plant has been found to exhibit various health benefits, including antioxidant, anti-inflammatory, and antimicrobial properties, which can aid in the treatment of wounds, infections, and other health conditions. Bioactive constituents of Lindernia ruelloides include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and glycosides, which contribute to its therapeutic properties. Traditionally, Lindernia ruelloides is used in herbal medicine, often prepared as a decoction, infusion, or tincture, and is also used in topical applications to treat skin conditions and wounds.
taxonomy: Lindernia ruelloides is a species of flowering plant in the family Linderniaceae. It belongs to the genus Lindernia, which consists of approximately 200 species of aquatic plants. Lindernia ruelloides is a member of the order Lamiales, a group of flowering plants that also includes mint, basil, and honeysuckle. The family Linderniaceae is a small group of flowering plants that are primarily found in tropical regions. Lindernia ruelloides is native to Africa, where it grows in aquatic environments.
